inline_comments.message: Add CM Write Data Job Scenario to K6 Test Suite
NB: Liek Java, clean code should not need comments
Do we need to handle this or is catching it enough?
NB Would a separate method to create the writeoperations be more readable?
should parellism not be handled by 'vus' parameter in the endurance .json file?

I think wrapping the values 10000 and 100 in another string based parameter is unnecessary and even hides useful details.
Can we not just call each method with the required numberOfOperations directly?
can you not simply define here as writeDataJobScenario(10000)
can you not simply define here as writeDataJobScenario(100)
Yes, "exec" represents the function to execute, so we could execute "writeDataJobLargeScenario", and "writeDataJobSmallScenario" in the other case.
Also, isn't the requirement 100000 (one hundred thousand) for large request?
above sounds good enough to me and a lot simpler then before

This will not match any alternate IDs that were set in registration. In cmhandle-crud.js, the alternate ID prefix we use is:

    Region=NorthAmerica,Segment=

Note the longest-match algorithm uses slashes, so we should change cmhandle-crud.js to use FDNs a alternate IDs

Also note, the generated paths above use ${i}, but for large operation we use 100000 operations, but we don't have 100000 CM-handles! It would be better to randomly generate a number between 1 and TOTAL_CM_HANDLES
This creates 3 write operations for each loop iteration. This means the check for the responses will be wrong, since we would get back 300 responses instead of 100.

Also need to add threshold for the new metrics here, e.g. 

    "write_small_data_job_duration": ["avg <= 2000"]
Nb. Would make sense to extract this to utils.js, since it needs the same string for `getRandomCmHandleReference`.
Blocker: You need to update this too to match new prefix, otherwise passthrough tests will fail.
This doesn't match the requirement of 100,000 total operations. We're doing 3x that. We expect 100,000 responses. See other comment about the payload for a solution (just do 2 operations per handle).
this is okay to be the same for all cm handles for your test?
NB: not needed since there is similar comments on line 56
NB: not needed since mentioned on line 56
it has been created twice. Thus I suggest make a constant of it in util.js then import and use.
NB: it does not used in multiple places but to have a constant of it in util.js would be nice.
This function is not needed since used only once
NB: Why did you divide it by 2. I don't understand.

NB: it is not clear what kind of kafka messages produced. 

Thus I suggest a proper name: produce_avc_event_kafka_messages

it is not clear what kind of data and where you are writing to.

You can be more specific e.g.: like A large volume of DCM write operation. The HTTP response code and expected number of reponses are verified.
Both have diff. value for redability its ok to have it here to specific JS file.
